title: Fix blank page on new tab/window open

requirements.summary: Fix blank page issue in App.tsx by ensuring session check on mount correctly reads localStorage and transitions to the appropriate page without rendering a blank screen
acceptanceCriteria: Opening the app URL in a brand new browser tab renders either the LandingPage (if no active session) or the ChatPage (if a valid session exists) — never a blank white or black screen.
The loading spinner (if shown) always resolves within a short time and transitions to the correct page.
The session check in App.tsx reads `globalchat_session` from localStorage synchronously or with a brief async check, and sets the correct page state before the first meaningful render.
The fix works consistently across Chrome, Firefox, and mobile browsers when opening a new tab.

file_operations.description: Refactor the session check logic to ensure it reads `globalchat_session` from localStorage synchronously on mount and immediately sets the correct page state (LandingPage or ChatPage) without leaving the app in a blank loading state. Wrap localStorage access in try-catch to handle corrupted data gracefully. Ensure the loading state resolves quickly and transitions correctly, preventing the app from remaining stuck in a blank state after the session check completes.

requirements.summary: Audit index.html and main.tsx to ensure React app mounts correctly on all page loads without silent failures
acceptanceCriteria: No JavaScript errors appear in the browser console on initial page load in a new tab.
The React app mounts successfully and renders content without requiring a page refresh.
The `root` div in `index.html` is correctly targeted by the React entry point.

file_operations.description: Verify that the root `<div id='root'>` element is present and correctly configured. Add error boundary markup or fallback content if needed to prevent silent mounting failures.

requirements.summary: Ensure auth.ts initializes synchronously without throwing errors by wrapping localStorage operations in try-catch blocks
acceptanceCriteria: The auth module loads without throwing exceptions even if localStorage contains malformed data.
Errors in `auth.ts` initialization do not prevent the React app from rendering.
The app renders correctly even if localStorage is empty (fresh browser profile or incognito mode).

file_operations.description: Wrap all localStorage reads and writes in try-catch blocks to prevent initialization failures from crashing the app. Ensure the `seedOwnerAccount` call and any other localStorage operations handle errors gracefully, returning safe defaults when localStorage is unavailable, corrupted, or empty (e.g., fresh browser profile or incognito mode).
